chapter_question: Why is Gaussian curvature intrinsic even when normals first define it?
inspection_targets: A bend from plane to cylinder preserves the metric and keeps K zero.
A rectangular grid bends without stretching lengths.
Stretching changes metric data; bending alone does not.
Curvature is computed from metric coefficients alone.

source_span.focus: States and motivates Gauss's discovery that Gaussian curvature is intrinsic despite being defined extrinsically.
source_span.label: Chapter 13
source_span.title: Gauss's Theorema Egregium
topics: Curvature preserved by bending without stretching
Consequences for maps and surface deformation
